chunk_summary: This section provides a worked example for staging IAP Class 2 and Class 3 obesity in children using standardized anthropometry and chart-based diagnosis, emphasizing accurate calculations and documentation for clinical decision-making.
learning_objectives: Understand the IAP obesity staging system for Class 2 and Class 3 using percent-of-cutoff calculations.
Learn how to use extended IAP tables for age- and sex-specific obesity thresholds.
Recognize the importance of accurate height and BMI measurements in pediatric obesity staging.
Identify clinical implications of Class 2 and Class 3 obesity staging for comorbidity screening and management.
key_facts: Class 2 obesity is defined as 120% to <140% of the adult-equivalent BMI cutoff (27 kg/m²).
Class 3 obesity is defined as ≥140% of the adult-equivalent BMI cutoff (27 kg/m²).
Example: A 13-year-old boy with BMI 31.2 kg/m² meets Class 2 obesity (125% of cutoff).
The adult-equivalent BMI cutoff for a 13-year-old boy is 24.9 kg/m², with Class 2 and Class 3 thresholds at 29.9 kg/m² and 34.9 kg/m², respectively.
Accurate height measurement is critical near thresholds to avoid BMI misclassification.
Higher obesity classes require intensified screening for complications like OSA, depression, and orthopedic pain.
reference_quote: Class 2 obesity is approximately ‡120% to <140% of the age- and sex-specific adult-equivalent 27 value, and class 3 obesity is ‡140%.
document_key_topics: Clinical implications of Class 2 and Class 3 obesity
